front: Author: title character enters her former maid's room, kills a cockroach, and puts its entrails in her mouth
back: Clarice Lispector (The Passion According to G.H.)

front: Author: poor typist Macabea is hit by a yellow Mercedes after visiting fortune teller Madame Carlota
back: Clarice Lispector (The Hour of the Star)

front: Author: 'The Smallest Woman in the World' — explorer Marcel Pretre discovers a 45-cm-tall pregnant Likouala woman
Author: former maid Janair drew charcoal drawings of a man, woman, and dog on her wall
back: Clarice Lispector (The Passion According to G.H.)

front: Author: debut novel published at age 23 about Joana and her adulterous husband Otavio
back: Clarice Lispector (Near to the Wild Heart)

front: Author: Macabea lives on hot dogs and Coca-Cola and learns facts from a Clock Radio
back: Clarice Lispector (The Hour of the Star)

front: Author: Ukrainian-born Brazilian Jewish woman compared to Virginia Woolf for stream of consciousness
Author: 'Love' — Ana breaks eggs on a tram when she sees a blind man chewing gum
Author: G.H. is a wealthy sculptor who compares her past abortion to squashing a cockroach
back: Clarice Lispector (The Passion According to G.H.)

front: Author: 'The Fifth Story' presents five increasingly bizarre accounts of cockroach killings
Author: Catherine says goodbye to mother Severina, who obsesses over her suitcases, in the title story of Family Ties
Author: Madame Carlota predicts Macabea will marry a wealthy foreigner named Hans
back: Clarice Lispector (The Hour of the Star)

front: Author: maid's room described as 'a violation of my quotation marks' making G.H. 'a citation of herself'
back: Clarice Lispector (The Passion According to G.H.)

front: Author: title of Near to the Wild Heart suggested from Joyce's Portrait of the Artist, which she had not read
Author: a dead dog found on a street corner is buried and then unburied by a mathematics professor
Author: narrated by Rodrigo, who chronicles the life and death of Macabea
back: Clarice Lispector (The Hour of the Star)
